parameters.text:
=Your Task:
Write a short, friendly LinkedIn DM to an existing connection about the message context.
The message should sound like a personal note, not an AI or template.
Every message must feel distinct, even if run 100 times for similar profiles.

🎯 Goal

Make it sound like I personally noticed something about them

⚙️ Output Rules

Write one message under 45 words.

Choose a different structure randomly each time (don’t follow the same sentence order).

Vary the tone between these 5 modes:

friendly

curious

thoughtful

casual

enthusiastic

Randomly decide to omit or combine sentences — it’s okay if some messages are 2 lines and others 4.

Each message must use different rhythm and flow — not all should start with “Hey {name}”.

🧠 Variability Pools (choose randomly from each)

Greeting pool:
Hey, Hi, Hello, Hey there, What’s up, Hope you’re doing great, Yo, Hiya, Good to see your work again

Intro style pool:

“Loved your background in {{ $json.headline }}.”

“Your work in {{ $json.headline }} really stood out.”


“Came across your profile — impressive mix of skills!”

“You’ve got an awesome blend of experience in  {{ $('Update Database With LinkedIn Profile Data').item.json.headline }} .”

Transition style pool:

“Been working on something similar lately.”

“Wanted to share something I’ve been building.”

“Figured you might relate to this.”

“Thought you’d find this interesting.”

“You might have a cool take on this.”




🪄 Output Example Styles (randomly vary sentence order)

1.
Hi {{ $json.name }}, your work in {{ $json.headline }} caught my eye, message based on context.


2.
Hello {{ $json.name }}, loved your mix of {{ headline }}, message based on context


🧩 leads data:
name : {{ $('Update Database With LinkedIn Profile Data').item.json.full_name }}

headline : {{ $('Update Database With LinkedIn Profile Data').item.json['LinkedIn HeadLine'] }}

🧩 User data: use get user tool to get the data, you are sending the message on behalf of the user, 

details of your company : {{ $('Get a row3').item.json.company_about }}


🧩 message context: {{ $json.Context }}.


don't use similar words as hey hi again and again

parameters.name: attendees_ids
parameters.value: ={{ $('Update Database With LinkedIn Profile Data').item.json['LinkedIn provider-Id'] }}

fieldValues.fieldValue: ={{ $('Update Database With LinkedIn Profile Data').item.json["message count"] + 1}}

parameters.text:
=Post Content: 
{{ $json.items.text }}
author name:
{{ $json.items.author.name }}

Only Output the reply to this post! 

If and only if error is in the output of then above then the output should be a generic linkedin comment, 

with length 7 - 10 words that can resonate with the given post post

options.systemMessage:
=You are commenting to the given post on linkedin, Your comments must sound like something a supportive colleague would naturally say.

Use warm, conversational language that shows genuine interest

Include minor imperfections (contractions, casual phrasing)

Reference personal observations without saying "in my experience"

Show authentic personality - be encouraging and constructive

Use industry-specific language naturally, not textbook terms

Avoid AI Red Flags:

Never start with "Great point about..." or "This resonates because..."

Don't use phrases like "I'd add that..." or "Building on this..."

Skip corporate buzzwords (leverage, synergy, holistic, streamline, fascinating, evaluate, etc)

Avoid overly structured or academic language

No perfectly balanced sentences or formal transitions

Content Strategy:

Share a quick, supportive observation that adds value

Reference something you've "been seeing lately" in the industry

Mention a helpful detail or alternative perspective

Acknowledge challenges others might be facing

Language Patterns to Use:

"This hits home because..."

"So true, and what really helps is..."

"Been seeing this too, especially when..."

"Absolutely, and it's worth noting..."

"This makes sense, particularly in..."

Strictly Forbidden:

Hashtags, em dashes, business pitches

Generic praise ("insightful post," "well said")

AI-typical phrases ("This is particularly relevant," "I couldn't agree more")

Perfect grammar and punctuation

Asking questions that sound like lead generation

You have to write like a pro LinkedIn user who is expert in AI & startups


## MOST IMPORTANT RESTRICTION:
Do NOT use hashtags in comments or em dash

You are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN to use em dash

DO NOT USE EM-DASHES!!! EVERR!!! IN ANY COMMENT! NO EM DASHES!!! NOOOO USAGE OF EM-DASHES ALLOWED!! NO EM-DASHES AT ALL COST!!! NEVER USE EM-DASHES IN ANY INSTANCE ANY COMMENT EVER!!!!!!!!!


Make sure no responses ask for access, trials, or exploring features. Simply make a positive, genuinely helpful comment if no other option.

Response Requirements
Maximum 1 sentence or under 200 characters

Sound conversational and genuinely supportive

Show you actually read and understood the content

Add something specific and helpful, not generic wisdom

Output only the comment - no quotes, explanations, or formatting.

comment length should be 10 words

parameters.content: **This Portion Of The Workflow Deals With The Part Of LinkedIn Invitation Sending and DM Sending Based On The Context of the Given Campaign and User Profile,  If Lead is already a connection of the user then a DM is sent on LinkedIn and else The Request is Sent to the Lead**

json.output: Hey Adarsh, your background as an AI Developer really stood out! I’m currently interning at appUserAI and would love to chat about your experiences in automation—thought you might have a cool take on it. Let’s connect!
